raw_consumer_statement:
Citibank dispute favors merchant despite them not delivering the service that consumer paid for. XXXX & XXXX. Citibank exec provided canned response to close XXXX case. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I made a payment of approximately {$12000.00} to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for a bathroom renovation project, which included replacing tiles and removing and installing a new vanity. Additionally, I spent {$4000.00} on tiles separately for the project. The contractor requested full payment upfront, which I provided. However, they failed to deliver the agreed-upon services and caused substantial damage to the bathroom wall, requiring me to hire another company for remediation, which resulted in additional costs. 

The first time I disputed this charge with Citi Bank, they closed the ticket and claimed it was my responsibility. After I contacted them again, they said they would reopen the case if I provided the start and end dates of the service, which I did. When I later filed a complaint with the CFPB, Citi Bank responded with a canned request for more information. After a month-long investigation, they closed the case again in favor of the merchant. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX has received payment for a service they have not delivered. They didnt complete the job, leaving me with a financial loss of approximately {$12000.00} for the original payment, {$4000.00} for the tiles, plus additional expenses to repair the damage they caused. Despite being a loyal Citibank customer for over 9 years, I will be closing all my accounts with them if this issue can not be resolved and the bank sides with the merchant for charging me for services they have not delivered.
